refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR is already focused on a single functional obligation: ensuring teleoperation communication quality meets a specified packet loss limit. It does not include multiple obligations or implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.

candidate_dps: Implement adaptive communication protocol to monitor and adjust transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within limits
Use error detection and correction mechanisms to recover lost packets and ensure communication quality
Design communication system with redundant data paths to mitigate packet loss
Incorporate real-time network quality monitoring and feedback control to dynamically optimize data flow
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement adaptive communication protocol to monitor and adjust transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within limits

potential_sources: Dependency on real-time network monitoring data
Interaction between adaptive protocol and error correction mechanisms if combined
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on a logical approach to maintain packet loss limits by adapting transmission parameters, which can be modularized independently from other system functions. Potential coupling with error correction or monitoring modules can be managed via clear interfaces.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to allow adaptive protocol effectiveness
Customer-defined packet loss limit is achievable with adaptive protocol alone
Operational policies do not restrict dynamic adjustment of communication parameters
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including real-time network quality monitoring as a separate DP or sub-DP to clarify modular responsibilities and improve independence.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for network communication management and protocol adaptation

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used to receive network status data and send communication performance metrics

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface to the physical or logical network layer for sending and receiving data packets

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as adaptive communication protocol is core to network handling; minimal diffusion since protocol adjustments are localized here.
Low containment as vehicle module focuses on physical and control systems; would require extensive diffusion to implement protocol adaptation.
Moderate containment since teleoperation depends on communication but protocol adaptation is not its primary responsibility.
Poor containment; safety services focus on alerts and safety monitoring, not communication protocol management.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing the adaptive communication protocol because it directly manages network communication and can encapsulate protocol logic with minimal diffusion to other modules.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on real-time telemetry data from other modules may require careful interface design to avoid coupling.
If network link interface changes, protocol adaptation logic may need updates, risking tight coupling.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by implementing an adaptive communication protocol that monitors and adjusts transmission parameters to maintain packet loss within the customer-defined limit, fulfilling the requirement for teleoperation communication quality.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on real-time telemetry data from other modules may require careful interface design to avoid coupling.
If network link interface changes, protocol adaptation logic may need updates, risking tight coupling.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and minimal diffusion risk, as the adaptive communication protocol is core to network handling and localized within this module. Other modules have low containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics via the Telemetry interface and ensuring compliance with the ≤1% packet loss acceptance criterion under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear and appropriate, with well-defined interfaces to Telemetry and Network Link supporting the implementation and verification of the FR.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly document interface contracts for Telemetry and Network Link to minimize coupling and manage dependencies.
Include contingency plans for interface changes to reduce tight coupling risks.
Consider adding monitoring and alerting mechanisms within the NGHE Solution module to promptly detect and respond to packet loss threshold breaches.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear verification feasibility, appropriate allocation, and manageable diffusion risks. The adaptive communication protocol implemented in the NGHE Solution module sufficiently fulfills the high safety criticality requirement of maintaining packet loss ≤1%. Recommended revisions focus on documentation and interface management rather than further decomposition, indicating the current level of decomposition is adequate.

revise.why: To explicitly document interface contracts and contingency plans to minimize coupling and manage dependencies, and to add monitoring and alerting mechanisms within the NGHE Solution module for prompt detection of packet loss threshold breaches.

assumptions: The adaptive communication protocol can be effectively verified via telemetry monitoring under agreed network conditions.
Interface changes can be managed through documented contracts and contingency plans.
open_risks: Potential dependency on real-time telemetry data may cause coupling if interfaces are not well managed.
Changes in network link interface may require updates to protocol adaptation logic, risking tight coupling.
evidence_needed: Verification data showing packet loss remains ≤1% under operational conditions.
Documentation of interface contracts and contingency plans.
Implementation of monitoring and alerting mechanisms within NGHE Solution module.
